question_text: A student measures a wave on a ripple tank. The distance between 5 consecutive wave crests is 20 cm. A stopwatch shows that 10 complete waves pass a fixed point in 4 seconds. Calculate: (i) the wavelength, (ii) the frequency, and (iii) the wave speed. Show all your working.
explanation: Between 5 crests there are 4 wavelengths (the distance between crests), so 4λ = 20 cm gives λ = 5 cm. Frequency is waves per second: 10 waves in 4 seconds = 2.5 Hz. Then use v = f × λ: v = 2.5 × 0.05 = 0.125 m/s. This multi-step problem requires converting observations into wave properties and combining all three quantities.
